text: Hello everyone, this is a video that is minimally edited of a XI-ETEP Reef Stopa Incisional Hernia Repair. This is an eighty-one year old female with a BMI of thirty-two. Her past medical history is significant for type two diabetes, fatty liver disease, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, osteopenia, a history of CVA, and facial nerve palsy. Her surgical history is significant for an open umbilical hernia repair in nineteen ninety nine and two thousand three. I'm not sure whether mesh was used then. She then had a laparoscopic incisional hernia repair with an eye palm mesh in two thousand fourteen and then in two thousand eighteen she underwent an open aortic endarterectomy and the operative note mentions that mesh was removed. She has developed several midline incisional hernias. The largest hernia is about nine by twelve centimeter, but above that she has multiple smaller defects within some diastasis. As you can see, the hernia contains some colon. Her rectus muscles are about eight to nine centimeters wide. The largest defect is in the lower half of the abdomen. in the N-three and N-four position. She is an ideal candidate for ETEP because her rectus muscle is nice and wide and also she has a very long laparotomy scar and incisional hernia which makes it difficult for other minimally invasive approaches.
